description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a long, cylindrical wooden handle and a single, broad metal blade. The handle is a natural wood color, smooth and straight, with a slight taper towards the end. The blade is dark, likely forged steel, and is securely mounted perpendicular to the handle, with a visible wedge or cap at the top for reinforcement. The blade has a classic wedge shape, with a sharp cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. There are no visible decorative elements or engravings, giving it a utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests it is intended for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or residential setting.
description: A hand axe with a straight wooden handle and a broad, dark metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The design is simple and utilitarian, with no visible markings.
description_view_0: The axe is seen at an angle, showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, dark blade attached at the top.
description_view_1: This view shows the axe from the top, highlighting the circular end of the handle and the width of the blade.
description_view_2: The axe is viewed from the opposite side, again displaying the full handle and the blade's profile, with the wedge and poll visible.
description_view_3: A direct side view of the axe, clearly showing the straight handle and the full profile of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth surface, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a sharp edge and flat poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible cap at the top of the handle.
